topic: Someone receives an invitation to their high school reunion
raw_output:
[story 1]

The envelope felt offensively thick in Liam’s hand, the heavy cardstock a tangible weight of obligation. He stared at the embossed script—'Class of 2005 Reunion'—and let out a low, almost inaudible sigh that seemed to vibrate in his chest. Another forced social engagement. He crumpled the invitation slightly, the crisp edges protesting under his thumb. He was already dreading the forced smiles, the stale anecdotes, and the sheer volume of people he actively wished to avoid. He tossed it onto the cluttered desk, the sound barely registering over the hum of the old desktop computer. He really needed a strong cup of coffee before facing another evening of manufactured nostalgia.

[story 2]

Maya picked up the glossy invitation, her fingers tracing the embossed lettering with an air of profound distaste. Her shoulders tensed almost imperceptibly beneath her sweater. *Of course,* she thought, a sharp, internal dismissal. Another mandatory gathering where she’d have to pretend she remembered everyone’s name and enjoyed the shared history. She folded the paper with unnecessary precision, smoothing out the creases as if she could iron away the unwelcome memory of the event. She shoved the invitation deep into a drawer, the metal scraping against other forgotten things. The thought of navigating small talk made her jaw clench tight.

[story 3]

When the mail arrived, David didn't even look up from the spreadsheet he was reviewing. The invitation landed on his worn wooden desk with a soft thud. He picked it up with two fingers, his expression remaining utterly neutral, bordering on severe. He scanned the details—date, time, location—and his brow furrowed slightly, a subtle tightening around his eyes that signaled deep displeasure. He didn't bother reading the accompanying notes; he simply folded the paper once, sharply, and placed it on top of a stack of bills. He wanted nothing more than to be anywhere else, doing anything other than pretending to be enthusiastic about reminiscing.
